rationale: Postcondition is fundamentally unprovable. The hypothesis only states r1@.map_values(|e| e@).to_set() == self_@.dom() and the same for r2, i.e., both Vec<EndPoint> have the same set-of-abstract-views. The conclusion det_keys_equal(r1,r2) is (r1 == r2), which for Vec<EndPoint> requires identical sequence ordering. Two vectors with the same set-of-views can have different orderings (e.g. [a,b] vs [b,a]), so set equality does not imply vec equality. Furthermore, the g_neq_tuple branch explicitly assumes !(r1 == r2), which together with a satisfiable hypothesis yields no contradiction. Tried: (1) direct assert(r1 == r2) under hypothesis (failed), (2) extensional set-equality bridge =~= (failed because asserted unconditionally), (3) case-split if hypothesis-then-bridge (bridge proves but postcondition still fails). No Verus axiom in vstd relates set-of-mapped-views equality to Vec equality, and no allowed proof technique can synthesize an ordering from a set.
